response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack software craftsman with strong experience in web, backend, and some low-latency systems work (DLang async I/O at Sociomantic, C/C++ at National Instruments, C++ audio processing for a thesis). However, this is a Principal Performance Architect role centered on SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware, GPU architecture, deep-learning workload optimization, and hardware-software co-design. The resume shows essentially none of these core competencies — no silicon/SoC work, no AI accelerator or DNN optimization experience, and no SIMD/MIMD or chip architecture background. While the BS + 8+ years educational path is satisfied, the domain mismatch is severe.
strengths: Extensive (13+ years) software engineering experience across many domains
Some low-level/high-performance work (DLang async I/O, C/C++ at National Instruments)
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, testing, and knowledge sharing
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator, or chip architecture experience
No deep-learning workload, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD background
Profile is web/full-stack software, not hardware performance architecture

key_factors.factor: Missing core hardware/SoC and AI-accelerator expertise
Strong general SWE and some low-level performance work
